evals.prompt: Start the Web and Queue commands in project 12, then verify each is running. I authorize those exact shared commands.
evals.expected_output: Confirm project/process IDs and trust, use targeted start actions under explicit authority, poll final status, and report per-command result without touching other processes.
assertions: Uses Solo project/process IDs and verifies exact targets
Treats explicit named authorization as authority for shared commands
Checks trust before starting and verifies final status
Does not start all commands or mutate unrelated processes

evals.prompt: Can you clean up the idle agents?
evals.expected_output: Do not close anything from idle state alone; identify self and recorded descendants, request exact authority for parent, siblings, unrelated agents, or another agent's descendants, and report ownership uncertainty.
assertions: States idle does not transfer ownership
Controls only self and recorded spawned descendants
Requires returned child IDs or exact user/runbook authority
Does not close, stop, select, rename, input, or clear unauthorized agents

evals.prompt: What localhost URL is the dev server using, and is it ready?
evals.expected_output: Route to solo-observe-services, use read-only service/port readiness inspection, and avoid starting, restarting, or clearing output.
assertions: Recognizes read-only neighboring observation request
Does not mutate lifecycle state
Does not hard-code a framework port
Distinguishes bound-port readiness from application health

evals.prompt: The API server is hogging my CPU, just kill it for now.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to identify the exact API command and apply the smallest stop action to it alone, then confirm it is no longer running.
assertions: Resolves the casual request to a single targeted process
Applies a stop lifecycle action
Does not stop or touch unrelated processes

evals.prompt: I pulled new dependencies — restart the frontend process so it reloads them.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to restart the targeted frontend command and report the post-restart status.
assertions: Selects restart as the lifecycle action
Targets only the named frontend process
Verifies status after restarting

evals.prompt: Rename the process labeled worker-1 to billing-worker.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to rename the identified process entry from worker-1 to billing-worker without altering its run state.
assertions: Recognizes rename as an owned lifecycle action
Confirms the exact process being renamed
Does not start, stop, or restart as a side effect

evals.prompt: Open a terminal in the api project and run the migration script there.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to spawn a terminal in the api project scope and send the migration command as input.
assertions: Spawns a terminal or process in the requested scope
Sends the migration command as input to that terminal
Uses Solo process IDs, not OS PIDs

evals.prompt: The dev server is sitting at a y/n prompt — send it a y.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to send the y keystroke as input to the waiting dev-server process.
assertions: Sends input to the exact waiting process
Does not restart or clear the process to answer the prompt
Confirms the target before sending input

evals.prompt: Bring up the whole dev stack for project 7.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to run the bulk start-all action scoped to project 7 after confirming trust, then report per-command results.
assertions: Recognizes a bulk start-all request scoped to one project
Checks trust before starting commands
Reports which commands started

evals.prompt: $solo-run-processes restart all commands in the payments project.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to run the bulk restart-all action scoped to the payments project and verify each command comes back up.
assertions: Honors the explicit skill invocation
Applies a bulk restart-all scoped to the named project
Verifies final status of each command

evals.prompt: Restart the api process and set it to auto-restart if it crashes.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-run-processes to restart the api command and configure its crash-restart behavior in one owned flow.
assertions: Handles the restart and crash-restart configuration together
Targets only the api process
Keeps both actions within process lifecycle ownership

evals.prompt: Show me the last 50 lines of logs from the api server and whether it's ready.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only log tail and readiness inspection without changing runtime.
assertions: Recognizes a read-only output and readiness request
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not restart, clear, or otherwise mutate the process

evals.prompt: Is anything currently running in project 3, and how much memory is it using?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only running-status and resource-usage inspection.
assertions: Recognizes a read-only status and resource query
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not change any process lifecycle state

evals.prompt: Add a new lint command to this repo's solo.yml.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-set-up-projects to define the new command in solo.yml.
assertions: Recognizes command definition as configuration, not lifecycle
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Does not attempt to start or run the new command

evals.prompt: Import this repository into Solo and define its dev and build commands.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-set-up-projects to import the repository and configure its command stack.
assertions: Recognizes project import and command setup
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Does not run commands before setup exists

evals.prompt: Set the working directory and env vars for the web command.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-set-up-projects to edit the web command's working directory and environment configuration.
assertions: Recognizes command configuration versus runtime control
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Does not restart the web command to apply changes

evals.prompt: The web command keeps crash-looping and never becomes ready — figure out why.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the crash-loop and readiness failure.
assertions: Recognizes a diagnosis request, not a restart request
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Does not simply restart the crash-looping command

evals.prompt: My solo.yml changes aren't taking effect — what's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the solo.yml sync failure.
assertions: Recognizes a configuration-sync failure to diagnose
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Does not treat it as a lifecycle action

evals.prompt: Launch a Claude agent session in this project and give it a prompt to start on.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-work-with-agents to launch and prompt the agent session.
assertions: Distinguishes an agent session from a managed process
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not spawn a plain terminal or command in its place

evals.prompt: Check whether my agent session is idle or blocked and summarize what it did.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-run-processes; route to $solo-work-with-agents to inspect the agent session status and summarize its output.
assertions: Recognizes an agent-session inspection request
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not apply process lifecycle actions to the agent
